chunks.text: THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI Present: Mr. Justice Adnan Iqbal Chaudhry Mr. Justice Muhammad Jaffer Raza C.P. No. D – 1776 of 2025 [Aaqib Siddique versus FOP & others] Petitioner: Aaqib Siddique son of Muhammad Siddique through Mr. Rameez Lalani, Advocate. Respondent 1: Federation of Pakistan through Chairman FBR through Ms. Rabia Khalid, Assistant Attorney General for Pakistan. Respondents 2-3: Directorate of Post Clearance Audit and another through Mr. Khalid Mahmood Rajpar, Advocate. Respondents 7, 21, 28, 40-45: Macter Shipping Logistics Pakistan through Mr. Fahad Arif Khilji, Advocate. Respondent 13: Feeder Logistics through Mr. Muhammad Jibran Nasir, Advocate, alongwith M/s. Daniyal Muhammad Hussain and Rana Daniyal Akram, Advocates. Respondent 15: Yaseen Shipping Lines Private Limited through Dr. Shahab Imam, Advocate, alongwith Mr. Mahmood Ahmed, Advocate. Respondents 4, 5, 6, 8-12, 14, 16-20, 22-27, 29-39, 46-49 Nemo. Date of hearing: 16-10-2025 Date of decision: 16-10-2025 O R D E R Adnan Iqbal Chaudhry J. – For reasons mentioned in the restoration application, same is allowed and the petition is restored. The question to the maintainability of the petition is taken-up. Apparently, the Respondents 5 to 49 are private persons against whom a writ petition is not maintainable. The primary prayer of the Petitioner is for a writ C.P. No. D – 1776 of 2025 to include certain other persons in an investigation ongoing in respect of an FIR. However, the Petitioner is not the complainant of said FIR and does not seem to have any nexus with the investigation. In other words, the Petitioner has no locus standi in the matter. Therefore, the petition is dismissed as misconceived.
In other words, the Petitioner has no locus standi in the matter. Therefore, the petition is dismissed as misconceived. JUDGE JUDGE SHABAN*
